title: syringa villosa subsp. wolfii
intro: Syringa villosa subsp. wolfii, also known as the Wolf's Lilac, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, native to China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Syringa villosa subsp. wolfii include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to help relieve coughs. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and combat various health conditions.
